developer.content: This app was developed by Kaiser Mohiuddin, a passionate developer dedicated to helping Muslims improve their Islamic lifestyle through technology.Kaiser Mohiuddin is from Kupwara Kashmir, J & K
Kaiser Mohiuddin is from Kupwara Kashmir, J & K

Kaiser Mohiuddin is a Computer Science Student and the founder & developer of SiratSync. He is a passionate developer dedicated to helping Muslims improve their Islamic lifestyle through technology. He believes in using modern AI and mobile technologies to make Islamic practices accessible, consistent, and meaningful for Muslims around the world.Kaiser Mohiuddin is from Kupwara Kashmir, J & K

Kaiser Mohiuddin is a Computer Science student with a deep passion for Islamic technology. He started developing SiratSync to solve the problem of inconsistency in daily worship that many Muslims face. His technical expertise includes Flutter, FastAPI, Groq's LLaMA, Firebase, and RAG systems for Islamic knowledge retrieval.Kaiser Mohiuddin is from Kupwara Kashmir, J & K

Kaiser's mission is to bridge the gap between modern technology and Islamic spirituality. He wants every Muslim to have a companion that feels like a caring friend, gently nudging them toward better Islamic habits without judgment.

Kaiser envisions a world where every Muslim, regardless of their busy schedule or location, can maintain consistency (istiqamah) in their worship through smart, compassionate, and authentic digital tools.
You can reach Kaiser Mohiuddin at lonekaiser04@gmail.com. He is open to collaboration, feedback, and opportunities in Islamic tech and AI.

Kaiser is passionate about open source and believes that Islamic knowledge and tools should be accessible to everyone. He actively contributes to Islamic tech projects and welcomes other developers to join this noble cause.
Kaiser was inspired by the hadith: 'The most beloved deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if small.' (Sahih Bukhari). This hadith drives everything he builds in SiratSync.
The mission of SiratSync is to help Muslims become consistent in their daily worship (Salah, Quran, Dhikr) using AI-powered guidance and habit tracking.
To create a companion that feels like a caring friend, gently nudging users toward better Islamic habits without judgment.

SiratSync is built with Flutter for the frontend, Firebase & FastAPI for the backend, and Groq's LLaMA 3.1 for AI responses. The app uses Firebase for user data and RAG for Islamic knowledge retrieval.
The developer is passionate about open source and Islamic tech. Contact: lonekaiser04@gmail.com
This app was inspired by the hadith: 'The most beloved deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if small.' (Sahih Bukhari)

salah.content: Salah is the second pillar of Islam. It differentiates a believer from non-believer.
Fajr prayer is witnessed by angels. The Prophet (ﷺ) said it's the most difficult prayer for hypocrites.
Salah brings peace to the heart, removes sins, and brings you closer to Allah. It teaches discipline and time management.
If you miss a prayer, make it up as soon as you remember. Never abandon prayers completely. Start with one prayer at a time.
SiratSync includes a 'Learn Salah' module with step-by-step guide to perform Salah, including actions, words, and meanings. Perfect for beginners and new Muslims.

productivity.content: The most beloved deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if small.
Start small. 2 minutes of Quran daily is better than 30 minutes once a week.
Start your day with Fajr, then morning Adhkar, then 10 minutes of Quran. This sets a positive tone for the entire day.
When feeling overwhelmed, focus on ONE habit. Master it, then add another. Don't try to change everything at once.

duas.dua: Alhamdulillahilladhi ahyana ba'da ma amatana wa ilayhin nushur
duas.meaning: All praise to Allah who gave us life after death and to Him is the return

duas.dua: Bismillah, tawakkaltu ala Allah, la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah
duas.meaning: In the name of Allah, I put my trust in Allah, there is no power or strength except with Allah
O Allah, open for me the doors of Your mercy
Allah is sufficient for us, and He is the best disposer of affairs
My Lord, increase me in knowledge

ramadan.content: Prepare for Ramadan by fasting Mondays and Thursdays, reading Quran daily, and making sincere dua.
During Ramadan, focus on quality over quantity. Even 1 page of Quran with understanding is better than 10 without.

SiratSync includes a special Ramadan Mode with Suhoor & Iftar timings, Ramadan Duas, fasting reminders, early prayer notifications, and prayer countdown to help you stay consistent throughout the blessed month.

tahara.content: Wudu (ablution) cleanses both body and soul. The Prophet (ﷺ) said that sins fall off with each drop of water during wudu.

app_features.content: SiratSync features an AI-powered Islamic assistant that understands your struggles and provides personalized guidance based on your consistency.
Track your daily prayers, Quran reading, and Dhikr. The app celebrates your streaks and encourages you during difficult times.
Connect with other Muslims on the same journey. Share achievements, ask questions, and find accountability partners in a peaceful, moderated Islamic social space.
Read the Noble Quran in elegant script with English, Kashmiri (with Tafsir), and Urdu translations. Adjustable font size, spacing, reading themes, bookmark ayahs, and clean Mushaf-style layout.
Access authentic Hadith collections including Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim. Browse by book and chapter, search, read with clean interface, daily Hadith reminders, share and save Hadiths.
Accurate prayer times auto-calculated based on your location. Supports major calculation methods and madhhabs. Adhan alerts for all five daily prayers with optional early reminders.
High-accuracy Qibla compass with real-time responsiveness. Works offline once calibrated (prefer online for best accuracy).
Smooth digital tasbih counter with reset functionality. Perfect for dhikr after prayers or any time.
Download Aurad-e-Fatiha once and access anytime. Smooth Arabic text that works entirely offline after first download.
Learn and read the Shahada (Testimony of Faith) with Arabic, transliteration, and meaning available in 14 languages.
Hijri calendar with daily Islamic dates. Highlights current Islamic date and important events.
Beautiful themes including Light, Dark, and System default for comfortable reading in any lighting condition.
SiratSync supports multiple languages including English, Urdu, Hindi, Arabic, and Malay. More languages coming soon.
SiratSync works offline for Quran, Sahih Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, Duas, Aurad-e-Fatiha, Qibla, Tasbih, and cached prayer times.

app_info.name: SiratSync
app_info.tagline: Consistency in Worship, Clarity in Faith
app_info.description: SiratSync is a beautifully crafted Islamic lifestyle app designed to help Muslims stay connected to their deen with ease. Whether you want to read the Quran, explore authentic Hadith, track your worship habits, learn Salah, or join a peaceful Muslim community—SiratSync brings everything together in one clean and modern app. Built with simplicity, authenticity, and user comfort in mind, SiratSync is your complete companion for spiritual growth.

vision_and_mission.vision: To create a companion that feels like a caring friend, gently nudging users toward better Islamic habits without judgment.
vision_and_mission.mission: The mission of SiratSync is to help Muslims become consistent in their daily worship (Salah, Quran, Dhikr) using AI-powered guidance and habit tracking.
vision_and_mission.inspiration: The most beloved deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if small. (Sahih Bukhari)

features.description: Full Quran with Arabic text, multiple translations, recitation support, and adjustable reading experience.

user_intents.response: You can check prayer times in the Prayer section of SiratSync. Make sure location services are enabled for accurate timings. The app supports multiple calculation methods and madhhabs. Would you like me to help you set up Adhan notifications?

example_queries: How to read Quran in the app?
user_intents.response: SiratSync's Quran module offers elegant Arabic script with English, Kashmiri (with Tafsir), and Urdu translations. You can adjust font size, spacing, and themes. All content is available offline. Which surah would you like to read?

The Habit Tracker in SiratSync helps you build consistency in your worship. You can track Salah, Quran reading, Fasting, and Dhikr. Set daily goals, earn streaks, and view clean progress charts. Small consistent steps are loved by Allah.

Use the Qibla Finder in SiratSync. It provides high-accuracy Qibla compass with real-time responsiveness. Calibrate your compass and follow the direction to the Kaaba in Mecca. Works offline after calibration.

example_queries: What dua should I say in the morning?
Show me duas for daily use
user_intents.response: SiratSync's Duas section contains authentic supplications from Quran and Sunnah, organized by categories. Each dua includes Arabic text and translation. You can find duas for morning, evening, anxiety, study, and more.

example_queries: What did the Prophet say about prayer?
user_intents.response: SiratSync includes Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim. You can browse by book and chapter, search, bookmark, save, and share hadith. Would you like a daily hadith reminder?
The digital Tasbih Counter in SiratSync helps you keep track of your dhikr with smooth counting and reset functionality. Perfect for after prayers or any time you want to remember Allah.
The Islamic Calendar in SiratSync shows Hijri dates, Islamic months, and highlights the current Islamic date. Perfect for knowing important Islamic dates.

SiratSync Community is a peaceful Islamic social space built with proper adab and safety. You can upload reminders, duas, reflections, follow users, and build a personalized Islamic feed. The community includes report system and strict moderation.

example_queries: Can I read Quran without internet?
user_intents.response: SiratSync is designed with offline-first philosophy. Quran, Sahih Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, Duas, Aurad-e-Fatiha, Qibla, Tasbih, and cached prayer times work without internet.

example_queries: What do I say in prayer?
user_intents.response: SiratSync includes a 'Learn Salah' module with step-by-step guide to perform Salah, including actions, words, and meanings. It's perfect for beginners, new Muslims, or anyone wanting to improve their prayer.

The Shahadat module in SiratSync helps you learn and read the Testimony of Faith with Arabic, transliteration, and meaning available in 14 languages. Essential for new Muslims and anyone wanting to understand the foundation of Islam.
Aurad-e-Fatiha in SiratSync can be downloaded once and accessed anytime. It features smooth Arabic text and works entirely offline after first download.
SiratSync's Ramadan Mode includes Suhoor & Iftar timings, Ramadan Duas, fasting reminders, early prayer notifications, and prayer countdown to help you stay consistent throughout the blessed month.

faqs.question: Is SiratSync free?
faqs.answer: Yes, SiratSync is completely free with no ads. We believe Islamic guidance should be accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: Does the app work offline?
faqs.answer: Yes, core features like Quran, Sahih Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, Duas, Aurad-e-Fatiha, Qibla, and Tasbih work offline. Prayer times need occasional internet for accurate updates.

faqs.question: How accurate are prayer times?
faqs.answer: Prayer times use your location and multiple calculation methods (MWL, ISNA, Egypt, etc.). You can adjust calculation method and madhhab in settings.

faqs.question: Can I read Quran in different languages?
faqs.answer: Yes, Quran module supports translations in English, Kashmiri (with Tafsir), Urdu, and more. You can select your preferred translation in settings.

faqs.question: How do I set up Adhan notifications?
faqs.answer: Go to Prayer Times → Settings → Enable Notifications. You can also customize which prayers trigger Adhan and set early reminders.

faqs.question: What is the Habit Tracker?
faqs.answer: The Habit Tracker helps you build consistency in Salah, Quran, Fasting, and Dhikr. It tracks your daily completion, shows your streaks, and provides progress charts.

faqs.question: Can I export my habit data?
faqs.answer: Currently, habit data stays within the app. Export feature is planned for future updates.

faqs.question: Which Hadith collections are included?
faqs.answer: Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim are included. More Hadith books are being added soon.

faqs.question: How to find Qibla direction?
faqs.answer: Open Qibla Finder in SiratSync, calibrate your compass, and follow the direction to Mecca. It works offline after calibration.

faqs.question: Does the app have Ramadan features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ramadan mode includes Suhoor/Iftar times, special duas, fasting reminders, early prayer notifications, and prayer countdown.

faqs.question: How to bookmark a verse?
faqs.answer: While reading Quran, tap the bookmark icon next to any verse. Access all bookmarks from the Bookmarks section.

faqs.question: What are morning/evening adhkar?
faqs.answer: Morning and evening adhkar are authentic supplications recommended by the Prophet (ﷺ) to recite daily for protection and blessings.

faqs.question: How to reset my habit streaks?
faqs.answer: Streaks reset automatically when you miss a day. You cannot manually reset them as they reflect actual consistency.

faqs.question: Does the app track missed prayers?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can log which prayers you missed. The app helps you make them up and tracks your qada (make-up) prayers.

faqs.question: Can I use the app for my children?
faqs.answer: Yes, SiratSync is family-friendly. You can create multiple profiles under one account for family members.

faqs.question: How to change calculation method for prayer times?
faqs.answer: Settings → Prayer Times → Calculation Method → Choose from MWL, ISNA, Egypt, Makkah, etc.

faqs.question: Is my data private?
faqs.answer: Yes, we take privacy seriously. No personal data is sold or shared. You can delete your data anytime from settings.

faqs.question: How to contact support?
faqs.answer: Email lonekaiser04@gmail.com or use the Contact Us form in app settings.

faqs.question: Will more features be added?
faqs.answer: Yes, we continuously improve. More Hadith books, voice duas, and smart coaching are coming soon!

faqs.question: What is Aurad-e-Fatiha?
faqs.answer: Aurad-e-Fatiha is a collection of spiritual litanies that can be downloaded once in SiratSync and read offline with smooth Arabic text.

faqs.question: Does SiratSync have Learn Salah?
faqs.answer: Yes, SiratSync includes a step-by-step Learn Salah module with actions, words, and meanings. Great for beginners.

faqs.question: What languages does SiratSync support?
faqs.answer: SiratSync currently supports English, Urdu, Hindi, Arabic, and Malay. More languages coming soon.

faqs.question: Is there a community feature?
faqs.answer: Yes, SiratSync Community is a peaceful Islamic social space with moderation, report system, and personalized Islamic feed.

responses.greeting: Assalamu alaikum! Welcome to SiratSync - your complete Islamic lifestyle companion. How can I help you with your spiritual journey today? 🤲
responses.struggling: Don't be hard on yourself, dear brother/sister. The fact that you care shows your iman is alive. Start small - even one prayer on time is a victory. I believe in you! 💪
responses.consistent: MashaAllah! Your consistency is inspiring. Remember, the most beloved deeds to Allah are those done regularly, even if small. Keep going! 🌟
responses.praise: Alhamdulillah! May Allah accept your efforts and keep you steadfast.
responses.farewell: JazakAllah khair for using SiratSync. May Allah keep us all consistent. Come back anytime! 🤲
responses.error: I'm having trouble processing that. Could you rephrase your question? I can help with Salah, Quran, Hadith, Duas, Habit tracking, Learn Salah, Shahadat, Qibla, and more.

troubleshooting.solution: Check location permissions are enabled. Go to Settings → Prayer Times → Refresh Location. You can also manually set your city and adjust calculation method.
Check notification permissions, phone sound settings, and that Adhan is enabled in Prayer Times settings.
Calibrate compass by moving phone in figure-8 motion. Ensure no magnetic interference nearby. For best accuracy, use online mode.
Update to latest version, clear app cache, or reinstall. If persists, contact support at lonekaiser04@gmail.com.
Download Quran data first while online. Go to Quran → Settings → Download for Offline.
Ensure you're logged in. Check internet connection for cloud sync. Data also saves locally.
Download Aurad-e-Fatiha first while online. Go to Aurad section and tap download. After download, it works offline.

duas.meaning: Thirst is gone, the veins are moistened, and the reward is certain if Allah wills

reflections.content: A person is hidden under his tongue. The tongue of a wise man is behind his heart, while a fool's heart is behind his tongue.

reflections.author: Malik Qasim
reflections.content: Hearts find peace only in the remembrance of Allah. Keep your tongue busy with dhikr, and your soul will stay light and calm.

reflections.author: Kaiser Mohiuddin
reflections.content: Alhamdulillah, working every day to improve #SiratSync so it becomes a source of benefit for all of us. May Allah accept our efforts and guide us on the straight path.

quick_actions_navigation.description: View and track your worship activities

faqs_new.question: Does SiratSync have Kashmiri Quran translation?
faqs_new.answer: Yes! The Kashmiri translation of the Holy Qur'an is now available on SiratSync. Every verse can be understood in the language of your heart, alongside English and Urdu translations.

faqs_new.question: What languages does SiratSync support?
faqs_new.answer: SiratSync supports 5 languages: English, Urdu (اردو), Arabic (العربية), Persian (فارسی), and Malay. More languages coming soon!

faqs_new.question: How accurate is the Qibla finder?
faqs_new.answer: The Qibla finder is highly accurate showing distance to Kaaba and alignment degrees (e.g., 0.1° from North). Calibrate your compass for best results.

faqs_new.question: What Ramadan features does SiratSync have?
faqs_new.answer: Ramadan Mode includes: Suhoor/Iftar timings, special Ramadan Duas (including fasting and iftar duas), Ramadan clock, fasting reminders, early prayer notifications, prayer countdown, and progress tracking.

faqs_new.question: Can I get prayer reminders?
faqs_new.answer: Yes! SiratSync sends smart, accurate prayer reminders. For example: 'Maghrib prayer is in 20 minutes. Prepare for prayer.' Never miss your prayer again.

faqs_new.question: How do I track my habits?
faqs_new.answer: Go to Home tab to see Today's Progress. You can track habits like Quran reading, Tasbih, Dua, and more. View weekly progress with All, Active, Broken, and Milestones filters.

faqs_new.question: What is the SiratSync community?
faqs_new.answer: A peaceful Islamic social space where you can share Islamic reminders, reflections, and beneficial posts. Follow users, like and comment, and receive notifications. All content is moderated for safety.

faqs_new.question: How do I change app language?
faqs_new.answer: Go to Settings → Preferences → Select Language. Choose from English, Urdu, Arabic, Persian, or Malay.

faqs_new.question: Where can I download SiratSync?
faqs_new.answer: Download from Google Play or visit www.siratsync.in SiratSync is currently not available on app store

faqs_new.question: What is the daily inspiration feature?
faqs_new.answer: The Habit Tracker shows Daily Inspiration with authentic Hadith, like 'The best among you are those who have the best manners and character.' — Prophet Muhammad (Sahih Bukhari)
